member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.BuiltInTypeSerializer`1.binaryReaderFunction
member.summary: The binary reader method appropriate for type T

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.BuiltInTypeSerializer`1.binaryWriterFunction
member.summary: The binary writer method appropriate for type T

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.BuiltInTypeSerializer`1.IsSupportedType
member.summary: Returns whether this class supports the provided type
member.returns: Whether the type parameter is supported

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.BuiltInTypeSerializerBuilder.MakeBuiltInTypeSerializer``1
member.summary: Returns an instance of where T is the data type passed in as parameter. The instance is returned as an object instance.

member.summary: Converts IEnumerable of byte[] to T

member.param: Writer to which the serialized data should be written.

member.summary: Indicates that the enumerator has completed.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableConcurrentQueueImpl`1
member.summary: Implementation for ReliableConcurrentQueue on native stack

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableConcurrentQueueImpl`1.#ctor(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Queue.TConcurrentQueue{`0},Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator)
member.summary: We need to make sure we only construct a new one if we never see it before. We need to maintain correct identity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableConcurrentQueueImpl`1.UnsafeCreateReliableConcurrentQueueImpl(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Queue.TConcurrentQueue{`0},Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator)
member.summary: Do not call this directly use GetOrCreate method instead We need to make sure we only construct a new one if we never see it before. We need to maintain correct identity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableConcurrentQueueImpl`1.GetOrCreate(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Queue.TConcurrentQueue{`0},Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator)
member.summary: Return or create a new ReliableConcurrentQueueImpl instance on given TConcurrentQueue instance. There is a 1:1 mapping through the Owner property.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableDictionaryImpl`2.#ctor(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.TStore{`1},Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator)
member.summary: We need to make sure we only construct a new one if we never see it before. We need to maintain correct identity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableDictionaryImpl`2.UnsafeCreateReliableDictionaryImpl(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.TStore{`1},Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator)
member.summary: Do not call this directly use GetOrCreate method instead We need to make sure we only construct a new one if we never see it before. We need to maintain correct identity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableDictionaryImpl`2.GetOrCreate(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Store.TStore{`1},Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.TransactionalReplicator)
member.summary: Return or create a new ReliableDictionaryImpl instance on given TStore instance. There is a 1:1 mapping through the Owner property.

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableDictionaryImpl`2.UpdateDictionaryChangeEvent(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Subscribe to store change callback in native layer or unsubscribe depending on the state change Must be called under a lock

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.Collections.ReliableDictionaryImpl`2.TestSetDictionaryChange(System.EventHandler{Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Notifications.NotifyDictionaryChangedEventArgs{`0,`1}})
member.summary: Enable DictionaryChange event without subscribing in native layer Useful in making sure we don't get DictionaryChange events when subscribing to Rebuild event

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.ReliableStateManager.ArbitraryUriPrefix
member.summary: Arbitrary Uri prefix to be prepended to non-Uri string names

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.ReliableStateManager._typeCache
member.summary: Mapping of reliable object types to concrete wrapper types.

member.name: T:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.StateManagerTypeCache
member.summary: Cache the concrete wrapper type corresponding to a given IReliableCollection interface in order to avoid reflection in subsequent calls to e.g. GetOrAddAsync

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.StateManagerTypeCache.defaultTypeTemplateMap
member.summary: Type template map for yielding default interface implementations

member.name: F:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.StateManagerTypeCache.typeCache
member.summary: Type cache to avoid subsequent calls to MakeGenericType

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.StateManagerTypeCache.GetConcreteType(System.Type)
member.summary: Get the wrapper type which corresponds to a given IReliableState interface

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.TransactionImpl.#ctor(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.Transaction)
member.summary: This needs to be private - we need to only construct if we've never seen this TransactionHandle before

member.name: M:Microsoft.ServiceFabric.Data.Extensions.TransactionImpl.GetOrCreate(Microsoft.ServiceFabric.ReliableCollection.Replicator.Transaction)
member.summary: Get an existing TransactionImpl if we've seen this TransactionHandle before. Otherwise create a new TransactionImpl instance.
